title: enable short-name aliasing
body:
Short-name aliasing was introduced with Podman 2.2 as an opt-in preview
by enabling an environment variable.  Now, as we're preparing for the
3.0 release, we can enable short-name aliasing by default.  Opting out
can be done by configuring the `registries.conf` config file.

Please refer to the following blog post for more details:
https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/container-image-short-names

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

The blog is a good start, but we'll need to document this further.  I'm not sure where yet but will noodle on it.

> The blog is a good start, but we'll need to document this further. I'm not sure where yet but will noodle on it.

Did you check `man containers-registries.conf`? We took great care to document everything there.
Yes, but my concern is someone running Podman might not think to look in registries.conf for more information.

> Yes, but my concern is someone running Podman might not think to look in registries.conf for more information.

Very good point! What do you think of adding a paragraph to `podman-pull` that further links to `registries.conf`?
I think the link should be in podman run/create as well, since that is likely the first place people will hit it.
